findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:413-464 contains an on-page, moment-by-moment depiction of Tom's escalation (not just Elsie's later report), including at least one direct physical contact/near-contact detail and one immediate consequence beat before the scene moves on; FAIL if the violence remains primarily summarized in dialogue.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the chapter's ugliest domestic-violence beat: the scene presents aftermath (hole in wall, burn) and retrospective explanation instead of rendering the violent escalation in scene-time. The narrative looks away at the moment consequence should be embodied.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the escalation window at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:413-423 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:459-464 so at least one concrete violent action beat is shown directly on-page (movement, impact, and immediate response), not only reported after the fact. Keep POV tight to Noreen while staging spatial positions (Tom/Elsie/sink/wall) and immediate bodily consequence.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two paragraphs in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:417-445 now contain explicit Noreen interior experience and each interior beat changes a subsequent line of dialogue or action; FAIL if the span remains external-only coverage.
findings.problem: Interiority dropout during peak pressure: this span runs as 10+ consecutive external-action/dialogue paragraphs with no clear focalizer interior thought/sensation/self-assessment, flattening close-third into screenplay mode.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:417-445, add 2-3 brief interior beats from Noreen (body signal, risk calculation, memory flash, or self-check) that materially shape the next spoken/action beat.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised Arthur lines in the cited spans sound primarily procedural under pressure and include at least one explicit limit/uncertainty beat; FAIL if he continues to deliver only clean de-escalation maxims without personal friction.
findings.problem: Arthur's speech repeatedly lands as polished rescuer rhetoric, edging into style-bible-forbidden savior dialogue. He solves leverage shifts with clean moral lines too consistently, which reduces friction and unpredictability.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Arthur's lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:301, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:403, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:537-539 into more task-first, less aphoristic speech. Add one beat where he misreads, hesitates, or states a practical limit.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised dialogue at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:553-561 includes at least two contractions in Noreen's lines and grounds her argument in immediate physical specifics of the house/lane; FAIL if the exchange remains abstractly rhetorical.
findings.problem: Noreen's final confrontation turns abstract and over-formal for her high-contraction, lived-in register under scene pressure ("Privacy was maintained by no one looking" / "I'm becoming observed").
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:553-561 so Noreen's pushback uses colloquial contraction-heavy rhythm and concrete house-level referents (gate bell, front-door route, wall tapping) instead of abstract nouns.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least four of the cited 'as if' instances are rewritten into direct-image/action lines and no cited paragraph stacks multiple comparator clauses; FAIL if the same comparator template still dominates those spans.
findings.problem: Recurring simile template tic: repeated 'as if ...' construction appears throughout the chapter and becomes a visible prose autopilot pattern rather than selective emphasis.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the cited spans (especially snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:7-9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:139-155, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:563, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:579), replace most 'as if' scaffolding with direct sensory consequence or concrete action.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited lines retain the same narrative meaning but no longer repeat the exact 'She did not ...' frame more than twice across those spans; FAIL if the repeated frame remains the dominant beat pattern.
findings.problem: A second recurring construction tic flattens rhythm: repeated refusal beats in the same sentence frame ('She did not ...') are overused and become mechanically emphatic rather than cumulative.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise refusal beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:111, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:157, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:303, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:573-575 by varying syntax and embodiment (action substitution, sensory interruption, interrupted thought) while preserving the secrecy motif.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited spans preserve Peter's controlling voice while delivering at least two control beats without accounting/discipline vocabulary and with distinct pressure mechanics (action/subtext rather than repeated ledger terms); FAIL if control remains mostly lexical bookkeeping repetition.
findings.problem: Mechanical repetition from over-literal style-bible token reuse: Peter's control voice repeatedly leans on accounting/discipline lexicon (columns/accounting/discipline/privacy bookkeeping) until characterization feels templated.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Peter-heavy passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:93-107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:185-223, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:545-559 to vary control tactics (social threat, timing pressure, bodily positioning, clipped contempt) without recurring to bookkeeping nouns each turn.

findings.acceptance_test: Read `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2437`-`snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2443`. PASS if the non-escalation choice is clear without generic stillness shorthand and remains psychologically legible. FAIL if the sentence remains a stock freeze beat.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book construction: default stillness shorthand appears in 7 chapters. In this chapter, `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2441` should REPLACE.
findings.rewrite_direction: At `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2441`, replace "went still" with a different defensive behavior under threat (e.g., deliberate non-response gesture, controlled repositioning) that better matches Noreen's suppression pattern in this scene.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the more specific and potentially uncomfortable rendering if it is character-true and causally necessary. Intimacy, bodily risk, and moral compromise must be shown with concrete detail and aftermath, then integrated into later behavior rather than quarantined as a one-scene shock.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft abstracts pain, sex, coercion, blood, or economic shame at the exact moment consequence should be embodied, revise by replacing summary with physically and socially specific action tied to POV stakes.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High by default for spoken lines; uncontracted forms are marked choices for period formality, emphasis, or character-specific control behavior.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural engines across adjacent chapters: ensemble social scenes, inventory chapters, information-turn chapters, acted-upon pressure chapters, and aftermath chapters. Vary scene count (2 to 4), dialogue density, and interiority depth so consecutive chapters do not share the same pacing signature.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-authentic but readable contemporary English, favoring concrete nouns and precise verbs over lyrical abstraction.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating primarily between Elsie and Noreen, with occasional tightly limited deviations only when causal clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Sentence rhythm should alternate compressed pressure bursts with longer observational lines that carry social texture; avoid monotone paragraph cadence.
